content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance ** Administration ** Executive/Legal ** Enforcement ** Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5660        322-5660           322-5991          322-6441            322-5444

March 16, 1981

Valerie A. Burrowes
City Clerk
350 Main Street
El Segundo, CA 90245

Re: Conflict of Interest Code
for the City of El Segundo

Dear Ms. Burrowes:

Ms. Milman requested that I respond to your letter. I
hope you find the following discussion helpful.

I reviewed the materials you sent, and as I understand
it, your questions are as follows:

1. Whether the Conflict of Interest Code for the
City of El Segundo must be amended to conform to 2 Cal.
Adm. Code Section 18730, the so-called standardized code;
and

2. Whether the City needs to amend its Code because
of the amendment to the Political Reform Act adding Planning
Commissioners to the provisions of Article 2 of Chapter 7,
Government Code Sections 87200, et seq.1/

1. I would like to clarify that cities are not required
to adopt the exact wording of 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18730
as part of their Conflict of Interest Codes; it is optional
and offered as a convenience since all future changes to 2 Cal.
Adm. Code Section 18730, which reflect changes in either the
Political Reform Act or Commission regulations, are automatically
incorporated. This precludes the necessity of amendments at
the local level every time there is a change in the law.
However, the substance of 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18730 is

1/ All statutory references are to the Government Code
unless otherwise stated.

Valerie A. Burrowes
March 16, 1981
Page Two


legally required to be in all Conflict of Interest Codes insofar
as these provisions embody the requirements of Section 87302.
In addition, I would like to mention that the Commission strongly
recommends the use of 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18730 by all
cities and counties.

The filing deadline contained in 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section
18730 is only suggested; Section 87302(b) states that the
filing deadline for annual statements shall be set in the
Conflict of Interest Code. If the City does not incorporate
2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18730, it may retain a deadline of
April 30.

2. Planning Commissioners were added to Section 87200
effective January 1, 1980. They are now required to file Form
721 by April 1 of each year. 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18723(b)(2).
Their disclosure obligations are set forth in the Act. Sections
87201, et seq. These requirements supersede any provisions of
a local Conflict of Interest Code. The change is effective
whether or not the Code for the City is amended. Certainly it
provides for greater clarity if it is so amended and is thus
advisable.

As Ms. Pritchard noted in her letter to Mr. Allen, Planning
Commissioners can remain in the Code for disqualification
purposes only.

Please feel free to contact me if you would like to discuss
this letter or if you have further questions.

Very truly yours,

Diane Mauri Fishburn
Counsel
Legal Division

DMF:pln

CITY OF
★ EL SEGUNDO ★

350 Main Street

El Segundo, California 90245

February 11, 1981

VALERIE A. BURROWES
City Clerk

Telephone
(213) 322-4670

Ms. Barbara Milman, Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Ms. Milman:

I have tried a few times unsuccessfully to reach you by phone in order
to discuss the matter which will make up the contents of this letter.
I fully understand that your schedule is a busy one, but sincerely hope
that you will find time to digest the text and perhaps make some determation of any action to be taken in the near future as the filing deadline is rapidly approaching.

We have been operating since 1976 with a city Conflict of Interest Code.
Now that Form 730 is mandated by FPPC, and with the change of the Planning
Commission having to file Form 721 rather than Form 730, I had questioned
my City Attorney with regards to amending:

(a) City Code to comply with FPPC Code

(b) Planning Commission Code to comply with FPPC Code

I am enclosing several attachments which may or may not help you to understand my questions of the matter.

(A) Recap sheet of evidence and present status of FPPC forms -vs-
City Codes

(B) Our Resolution No. 2759 establishing filing date

(C) Letter to Planning Commission from City Attorney, dated
January 15, 1980

(D) My memorandum to the City Attorney, dated January 12, 1981

(E) Memorandum from City Attorney to me dated January 13, 1981

(F) Letter from Jeanne Pritchard to City Attorney dated
January 16, 1981

FPPC Ms Barbara Milman                    -2-                    February 11, 1981


My main concerns are:

     (a)  Our Code presently does not conform to the FPPC Code,
          i.e.  wording and filing deadline

     (b)  Planning Commission Code is presently covered under the
          City Conflict of Interest Code assuming that they would
          file Form 730, and in addition, it shows their filing
          deadline as April 30

Do we need to amend our Code?  If so, who, what, where.  I spoke with
Ms. Pritchard last week with regards to her conversation and letter to
the City Attorney, Mark Allen.  During our conversation, she stated that
you would be responsible for the ruling as she stated it to him and she
felt I should take the matter up with you.

I would appreciate your findings and/or ruling in writing to me as soon
as possible.

                                             Sincerely,

                                             [signature]
                                             Valerie A. Burrowes
                                             City Clerk of the City of
                                             El Segundo, California

VAB:bb
Encl.

STATEMENTS OF ECONOMIC INTERESTS


12-7-76         Resolution #2728 adopted Code for Planning Commission

12-15-76        Conflict of Interest Code Planning Commission filed with City Clerk

1-15-77         Conflict of Interest Codes for Departments filed with City Clerk

2-15-77         Resolution #2744 adopted Code for Departments

3-15-77         Resolution #2759 adopted, fixes time of April 30 of each year for
                filing deadline for Statements of Economic Interests
                (for any agency the City Council is the Code Reviewing Body)
                includes all designated employees and Planning Commissioners

10-3-78         Codes for each department amended changing deadline filing date
                from "during month of February" to "during month of April"
                (Planning Commission was omitted from list)

1-2-80          Codes of following departments amended to delete and add classifications
                required to file:

                    City Manager's Office          Library
                    Building Department            Planning Department
                    Finance Department             Public Works Department
                    Fire Department


                STATUS OF FPPC FORMS & CITY CODES

                     Form 730              Form 721              City Code            Planning
                                                                                      Commission Code

Assumed         30 days after        Appointed Officers:       a) 30 days after     10 days before
Position        assuming position     10 days after taking       assuming position    assuming office
                                     office
                                                                b) Dept. Heads:
                                                                10 days before
                                                                assuming position

Annual          No later than        At time prescribed       April 30, per Reso.   April 30 per
Statements      April 1              by Agencies' Codes       No. 2759              Reso. #2759
                                                              Dept. Codes amended
                                                              Oct. 3, 1978
                                                              (No covering Reso.)

Leaving         within 30 days       within 30 days          No mention            No mention
Office          after leaving        after leaving

City of El Segundo

350 Main Street
El Segundo, California 90245
(213) 322-4670

MARK C. ALLEN, JR
CITY ATTORNEY

January 15, 1980


Planning Commission
City of El Segundo
California

Re: Conflict of Interest Code--Filing Annual Statements

Gentlemen:

Under the existing law you are required to file your annual report
that is due in 1980 under the local Conflict of Interest Code. I
have asked the City Clerk to furnish you with the forms. Under that
Code filing is required in February.

Starting next year, members of the Planning Commission will be required
to file under State law. This will also necessitate the repeal of your
existing Code and the adoption of a much simplified local code of the
Planning Commission, which will be furnished to you for your consideration before the end of 1980.

Respectfully submitted,

Mark C. Allen, Jr.
City Attorney

MCA:ps

cc: City Clerk ✓

CITY OF EL SEGUNDO

INTER-DEPARTMENTAL CORRESPONDENCE

Date January 12, 1981

To: Mark Allen

From: Val Burrowes

Subject: Conflict of Interest Code re: Planning Commission

I am somewhat concerned that we are still sitting at present with an
old Code for the above group.

I am attaching a copy of your letter dated 1/15/80.

There are some changes with FFPC now that the designated employees
are to file their mandated form #730 and I am wondering if all Codes
need to be amended?

Val

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662                322-5660              322-5901            322-6441            322-6444


January 16, 1981


Mark Allen
City Attorney
350 Main Street
El Segundo, CA  90245

Dear Mr. Allen:

     To follow up on our telephone conversation of a couple
days ago, the City of El Segundo is not required at this time
to amend its Conflict of Interest Code on file with the Fair
Political Practices Commission to include those city officials
recently designated under Article 2 of Chapter 7 of the Political
Reform Act.  The Article 2 filers should remain in the City's
Conflict of Interest Code for disqualification purposes only.

     There is still one remedy for violation of the conflict
of interest provisions of a code that is not provided for a
violation of Section 87100.  See Section 91005(b).  We are
considering proposing an amendment to Section 91005(b) to pro-
vide a remedy for violations of Section 87100.  Once the legis-
lation is passed, disqualification codes for Article 2 filers
will no longer be required.

     Please call me if you have any questions.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Jeanne Pritchard
                                        Administrative Assistant

12237

RESOLUTION NO. 2759

A RESOLUTION OF THE CITY COUNCIL OF THE

CITY OF EL SEGUNDO, CALIFORNIA, FIXING

THE TIME FOR REPORTS UNDER THE POLITICAL

REFORM ACT OF 1974.

THE CITY COUNCIL OF THE CITY OF EL SEGUNDO,

CALIFORNIA, DOES RESOLVE AS FOLLOWS:

SECTION 1. The City Council does hereby fix the closing

date for reports for all officers for whom the City Council is the

Code Reviewing Body as December 31, 1976, and each December 31

thereafter. The City Council further fixes April 30, 1977, and

April 30 of each subsequent year, as the deadline for filing

Statements of Economic Interest for any agency for which the City

Council is the Code Reviewing Body.

SECTION 2. The City Clerk shall certify to the passage

and adoption of this resolution; shall enter the same in the

book of original resolutions of said city; and shall make a

minute of the passage and adoption thereof in the records of the

proceedings of the City Council of said city, in the minutes of

the meeting at which the same is passed and adopted.

PASSED, APPROVED and ADOPTED this _15th___ day of

_March_____________, 1977.


Mayor of the City of El Segundo,
California

ATTEST:

City Clerk

(SEAL)

CITY OF EL SEGUNDO

INTER-DEPARTMENTAL CORRESPONDENCE

Date January 13, 1981

To: Mrs. Valerie A. Burrowes, City Clerk

From: Mr. Mark C. Allen, Jr., City Attorney

Subject: Planning Commission's Conflict of Interest Code

The problems raised by the Planning Commission's Conflict of Interest Code
were discussed today with Mrs. Pritchard of the legal staff of the Fair
Political Practices Commission. Ms. Pritchard advised me that we should
not take steps to correct that Code until a mailing from FPPC has been
received by the City Clerks. That mailing should reach the Clerks, accord-
ing to Ms. Pritchard, within three weeks.

When you receive the mailing from FPPC would you please be kind enough to
make a copy and forward it to me.

Mark C. Allen, Jr.
City Attorney

MCA:ps

sections.question_synthetic: The City of El Segundo seeks FPPC guidance on whether it must amend its Conflict of Interest Code to comply with new FPPC requirements regarding Form 730 for designated employees and Form 721 for Planning Commission members, particularly regarding filing deadlines and code structure.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request document to FPPC, not an FPPC advice letter response. The document contains the original request letter dated February 11, 1981, along with supporting attachments and related correspondence. One FPPC response from Jeanne Pritchard dated January 16, 1981 is included, but this is not the primary FPPC response to the main inquiry. The main request lacks a formal QUESTION/CONCLUSION section structure.

embedding.summary: City Clerk Valerie Burrowes requests FPPC guidance on amending the City of El Segundo's Conflict of Interest Code to comply with updated FPPC requirements, including Form 730 filing for designated employees and Form 721 for Planning Commission members. The document includes supporting materials showing the evolution of filing requirements and codes from 1976 to 1981, along with related correspondence from the City Attorney and FPPC staff member Jeanne Pritchard.
